Power plan supports 4 BYOK providers: OpenRouter, Groq, Google Gemini, Anthropic
Post-Fix Design Improvements Applied in One Audit Pass

nodes.label: Geo/Currency Cookie Logic
nodes.rationale: Reads x-vercel-ip-country header to set country/currency cookies so downstream components and APIs can serve INR vs USD pricing without repeated geo-lookups.

nodes.label: Auth Route Guard Pattern
nodes.rationale: Protects /dashboard, /optimize, /settings by redirecting unauthenticated users to /login with a 'next' param, and redirects already-logged-in users away from /login to /dashboard.

nodes.label: AI Provider Priority Logic
nodes.rationale: Priority order: 1) Anthropic env key (highest quality), 2) User BYOK key from DB, 3) Env fallbacks (GROQ, OPENROUTER, GEMINI). Allows the platform to work without an Anthropic key while supporting user-controlled AI provider via BYOK.

nodes.label: BYOK (Bring Your Own Key) Pattern
nodes.rationale: Users supply their own AI provider API keys (OpenRouter, Groq, Gemini) encrypted with AES-256 and stored in byok_keys table. Keys are decrypted server-side only at point of use, never logged or exposed. BYOK plan tier is the cheapest paid subscription.

nodes.label: PDF/DOCX Text Extraction (pdf-parse, mammoth)
nodes.rationale: Uses pdf-parse for PDF and mammoth for DOCX text extraction. Both are loaded as serverComponentsExternalPackages in next.config.mjs to avoid bundling issues.

nodes.label: Login Page (/login)
nodes.description: Baseline screenshot of the login page. Centered single-card layout on #F9FAFB background. Contains logo above card, auth card, and back-to-home link below. Card has 12px border radius, white background, light border.

nodes.label: ATSBYOK Logo Lockup (Login Page)
nodes.description: Logo appears above the auth card, vertically centered in the upper area. Green square icon with document glyph followed by 'ATS' in #111827 bold and 'BYOK' in #1D9E75 bold. Annotated as @c1 (icon), @c3 (ATS text), @c4 (BYOK text) in login-annotated.png.

nodes.label: Logo Icon — Green Square Document Glyph
nodes.description: Green (#1D9E75) rounded-square icon with a white document/file glyph. Annotated as @c1 in login-annotated.png. Positioned left of the wordmark text.
nodes.norm_label: logo icon — green square document glyph

nodes.label: Logo Text 'ATS' (dark)
nodes.description: The 'ATS' portion of the wordmark rendered in #111827 (text-primary dark). font-weight 700. Annotated as @c3 in login-annotated.png.

nodes.label: Logo Text 'BYOK' (green)
nodes.description: The 'BYOK' portion of the wordmark rendered in #1D9E75 (primary green). font-weight 700. No space between ATS and BYOK. Annotated as @c4 in login-annotated.png.

nodes.label: Auth Card Container
nodes.description: White card with rounded corners (12px border radius), light #E5E7EB border, centered on page. Contains heading, subheading, Google OAuth button, legal consent text, and three trust badges.

nodes.label: Welcome Back Heading
nodes.description: H1-style text 'Welcome back' in #111827, bold, centered at top of auth card.

nodes.label: Login Card Subheading
nodes.description: Secondary text 'Sign in to continue optimizing your resume' in #6B7280, centered below the heading. Reinforces product purpose at the auth gate.

nodes.label: Continue with Google — OAuth Button
nodes.description: Full-width button with Google 'G' multicolor logo and label 'Continue with Google'. White background, border, 8px border radius. Only auth method — no email/password. Annotated as @e2 in login-annotated.png. Highlighted in red with pink background in annotated version indicating an issue or point of interest.

nodes.label: Google 'G' Brand Icon on OAuth Button
nodes.description: Google's official multicolor 'G' icon displayed inline-left on the Google OAuth button. Standard Google brand asset.
nodes.norm_label: google 'g' brand icon on oauth button

nodes.label: Legal Consent Microcopy
nodes.description: Small text below the Google button: 'By continuing, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Policy.' Terms and Privacy Policy are hyperlinks in #1D9E75.

nodes.label: Terms of Service Link (login consent)
nodes.description: Inline hyperlink 'Terms' in #1D9E75 green within the consent text. Links to /terms. Annotated as @e3 in login-annotated.png.

nodes.label: Privacy Policy Link (login consent)
nodes.description: Inline hyperlink 'Privacy Policy' in #1D9E75 green within the consent text. Links to /privacy. Annotated as @e4 in login-annotated.png.

nodes.label: Trust Badges Row (3-column)
nodes.description: Three equally-spaced trust signal items at the bottom of the auth card. Displayed in a horizontal row. Baseline (login.png) uses emoji icons; after-login.png uses outline SVG icons — indicating a design change was applied.

nodes.label: Trust Badge: AES-256 Encrypted
nodes.description: First trust badge: lock emoji (baseline) / outline lock SVG (after-login) + text 'AES-256 encrypted'. Communicates security of stored API keys. Maps to BYOK encryption feature.

nodes.label: Trust Badge: Instant Access
nodes.description: Second trust badge: lightning bolt emoji (baseline) / outline lightning SVG (after-login) + text 'Instant access'. Communicates no waiting period after sign-in.

nodes.label: Trust Badge: Free to Start
nodes.description: Third trust badge: credit card / tag emoji (baseline) / outline tag SVG (after-login) + text 'Free to start'. Communicates the free plan exists, reducing friction at auth gate.

nodes.label: Back to Home Link
nodes.description: '← Back to home' text link in #9CA3AF (muted) below the auth card. Allows exit from login flow back to landing page. Annotated as @e5 in login-annotated.png. Navigation target: /.

nodes.label: Login Page — Annotated Screenshot
nodes.description: Design audit annotation overlay on login page. Red badges mark interactive and brand elements: @c1 (logo icon), @c3 (ATS text), @c4 (BYOK text), @e2 (Google OAuth button — highlighted pink background), @e3 (Terms link), @e4 (Privacy Policy link), @e5 (Back to home link). The Google button annotation includes a pink/red background suggesting it was flagged as a design issue or interactive concern.

nodes.label: Login Page — After Design Fix (after-login.png)
nodes.description: Post-fix screenshot of login page. Structurally identical to login.png but trust badge icons changed from emoji to outline SVG icons (lock, lightning bolt, tag). Trust badge text color changed from dark to #9CA3AF muted. Represents the 'after' state of a design audit fix.

nodes.label: Design Decision: Google-Only Authentication
nodes.description: Login page uses only Google OAuth — no email/password, no other providers. Single auth method simplifies UX and removes password management complexity. Aligns with Supabase Google provider setup.

nodes.label: Design Change: Emoji to SVG Trust Badge Icons
nodes.description: Between login.png (before) and after-login.png (after), trust badge icons changed from OS-rendered emoji (🔒 ⚡ 💳) to consistent outline SVG icons. Text color simultaneously shifted to muted #9CA3AF. This change improves cross-platform visual consistency and professional polish.
nodes.norm_label: design change: emoji to svg trust badge icons

nodes.label: Layout Pattern: Centered Auth Card on Full-Page Background
nodes.description: Login page uses a single centered card layout on a #F9FAFB full-page background. No sidebar, no split-screen, no background imagery. Logo is above the card, back-link is below. Consistent with minimal SaaS auth patterns.
nodes.norm_label: layout pattern: centered auth card on full-page background

nodes.label: Design Decision: No Navbar on Login Page
nodes.description: The login page does not show any navbar (PublicNavbar, AppNavbar, or SmartNavbar). Navigation is replaced by the logo above the card and the back-to-home link below. This is a deliberate choice to reduce distraction during the auth flow.
nodes.norm_label: design decision: no navbar on login page
